PHS 398 DETAILED BUDGET FOR INITIAL BUDGET PERIOD (Form Page 4)
DIRECT COSTS ONLY

A. Senior/Key Personnel                          $182,000
   1. Margaret L. Chen, Ph.D. (PI) - 30% effort    $68,400
   2. James R. Whitfield, M.D., Ph.D. - 15% effort $42,600
   3. Sarah K. Okonkwo, Ph.D. - 10% effort         $36,000
   4. David A. Petrov, Ph.D. - 10% effort           $35,000

B. Other Personnel                                $62,000
   1. Postdoctoral researcher (TBN) - 100%          $56,484
   2. Undergraduate students (2) - hourly           $5,516

C. Equipment (Itemize)                            $0

D. Travel                                        $8,500
   1. Domestic: American Heart Association Meeting  $5,000
   2. International: None                           $3,500

E. Participant/Trainee Support Costs              $0

F. Other Expenses                                 $152,500
   1. Genomic sequencing services                   $95,000
   2. Cell culture and reagent supplies             $28,000
   3. Computational cloud computing                 $18,000
   4. Publication and open access fees              $5,500
   5. Animal facility costs                         $6,000

G. TOTAL DIRECT COSTS FOR INITIAL BUDGET PERIOD   $405,000

H. INDIRECT COSTS
   Type: MTDC   Rate: 54.5%   Base: $405,000
   Indirect Costs Year 1: $176,250

I. TOTAL COSTS YEAR 1 (Direct + Indirect):        $581,250

BUDGET FOR ENTIRE PROPOSED PROJECT PERIOD
Total Direct Costs: $2,025,000
Total Indirect Costs: $881,250
Total Project Costs: $2,906,250

---

SPECIFIC AIMS

Heart failure (HF) affects approximately 6.5 million adults in the United States, with an annual incidence
exceeding 900,000 new cases. Despite advances in pharmacological and device-based therapies, the
5-year mortality rate remains above 50%. A critical knowledge gap persists in understanding the genomic
mechanisms that drive the transition from compensated cardiac hypertrophy to decompensated heart
failure.

Aim 1: Identify novel genomic variants associated with cardiac remodeling trajectories using
whole-genome sequencing of 2,500 heart failure patients from the Wisconsin Heart Failure Registry.

Aim 2: Characterize the functional impact of top candidate variants on cardiomyocyte gene expression
and chromatin architecture using single-cell multiomics approaches.

Aim 3: Develop and validate a polygenic risk score for heart failure progression that integrates
genomic, transcriptomic, and clinical predictors.

expected_values.Study Section: Genomics, Computational Biology and Technology Study Section (GCAT)
expected_values.Project Title: Genomic Determinants of Cardiac Remodeling in Heart Failure Progression
expected_values.Specific Aims: Aim 1: Identify novel genomic variants associated with cardiac remodeling trajectories using whole-genome sequencing of 2,500 heart failure patients from the Wisconsin Heart Failure Registry. Aim 2: Characterize the functional impact of top candidate variants on cardiomyocyte gene expression and chromatin architecture using single-cell multiomics approaches. Aim 3: Develop and validate a polygenic risk score for heart failure progression that integrates genomic, transcriptomic, and clinical predictors.

B. Other Personnel                                $48,000

E. Participant/Trainee Support Costs              $0

F. Other Expenses                                 $38,400
   1. Viral vector production (AAV-ChR2, AAV-NpHR)  $12,000
   2. Stereotaxic surgery supplies                   $8,400
   3. Histology and immunostaining reagents          $6,500
   4. Animal purchase (C57BL/6J mice, n=120)         $7,200
   5. Animal per diem and veterinary care            $4,300

   Type: MTDC   Rate: 57%    Base: $146,500

CUMULATIVE BUDGET
Year 1 Direct Costs: $175,000     Year 2 Direct Costs: $100,000
Total Direct Costs: $275,000
Total Indirect Costs: $143,000
Total Project Costs: $418,000

---

SPECIFIC AIMS

Post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) affects approximately 6-8% of the U.S. population, with
impaired fear extinction being a hallmark feature. The infralimbic (IL) prefrontal cortex and
basolateral amygdala (BLA) are critical nodes in extinction circuits, yet the precise cell-type-specific
mechanisms by which IL projections to BLA regulate extinction consolidation remain unknown.

Aim 1: Determine whether optogenetic activation of IL-to-BLA projection neurons during extinction
training enhances extinction memory consolidation in a mouse model of PTSD.

Aim 2: Test whether optogenetic silencing of IL-to-BLA projections during extinction recall
impairs the retrieval of extinction memory and promotes fear renewal.

expected_values.Project Title: Optogenetic Dissection of Prefrontal-Amygdala Circuits in Fear Extinction
expected_values.Specific Aims: Aim 1: Determine whether optogenetic activation of IL-to-BLA projection neurons during extinction training enhances extinction memory consolidation in a mouse model of PTSD. Aim 2: Test whether optogenetic silencing of IL-to-BLA projections during extinction recall impairs the retrieval of extinction memory and promotes fear renewal.

PHS 398 DETAILED BUDGET FOR INITIAL BUDGET PERIOD (Form Page 4)
DIRECT COSTS ONLY

Budget Period: 01/01/2025 - 12/31/2025 (Year 1)

A. Senior/Key Personnel                          $37,500
   1. Kirsten E. Johansson (PI) - 25% effort       $22,500
   2. Thomas J. Rearden (Co-I) - 10% effort         $15,000

B. Other Personnel                                $24,000
   1. Undergraduate research assistants (4) - hourly $24,000

C. Equipment (Itemize)                            $0

D. Travel                                        $5,400
   1. Society of Environmental Toxicology Meeting   $3,200
   2. Field sampling site travel (Northeast Missouri) $2,200

E. Participant/Trainee Support Costs              $0

F. Other Expenses                                 $33,100
   1. LC-MS/MS analytical services                   $14,000
   2. ELISA kits (vitellogenin, E2, T3/T4)          $6,800
   3. Field sampling equipment and supplies          $5,400
   4. Histology and microscopy supplies              $4,200
   5. Statistical software licenses                  $2,700

G. TOTAL DIRECT COSTS FOR INITIAL BUDGET PERIOD   $100,000

H. INDIRECT COSTS
   Type: MTDC   Rate: 45%    Base: $100,000
   Indirect Costs Year 1: $45,000

I. TOTAL COSTS YEAR 1:                            $145,000

CUMULATIVE BUDGET
Year 1: $100,000    Year 2: $100,000    Year 3: $100,000
Total Direct Costs: $300,000
Total Indirect Costs: $135,000
Total Project Costs: $435,000

---

SPECIFIC AIMS

Microplastic pollution is now ubiquitous in freshwater ecosystems, with concentrations in some
midwestern streams exceeding 100 particles per liter. Emerging evidence suggests that microplastics
can act as vectors for endocrine-disrupting chemicals (EDCs), yet the ecological consequences for
wild fish populations remain poorly characterized, particularly in agricultural watersheds.

Aim 1: Quantify microplastic abundance and polymer composition in three freshwater streams
in northeast Missouri representing a gradient of agricultural land use intensity.

Aim 2: Measure endocrine disruption biomarkers (plasma vitellogenin, sex steroid hormones,
thyroid hormones) in resident creek chub (Semotilus atromaculatus) populations and correlate
with microplastic exposure levels.

Aim 3: Engage 12 undergraduate researchers in an authentic course-based research experience
(CURE) integrating environmental sampling, analytical chemistry, and ecotoxicology.

expected_values.Project Title: Microplastic-Mediated Endocrine Disruption in Freshwater Fish Populations
expected_values.Specific Aims: Aim 1: Quantify microplastic abundance and polymer composition in three freshwater streams in northeast Missouri representing a gradient of agricultural land use intensity. Aim 2: Measure endocrine disruption biomarkers (plasma vitellogenin, sex steroid hormones, thyroid hormones) in resident creek chub (Semotilus atromaculatus) populations and correlate with microplastic exposure levels. Aim 3: Engage 12 undergraduate researchers in an authentic course-based research experience (CURE) integrating environmental sampling, analytical chemistry, and ecotoxicology.
